title: 16 Viewing Deposits Online and Tracing Deposits
body_text:
16	Viewing Deposits Online and Tracing Deposits
16.1	Overview of Viewing Deposits Online and Tracing Deposits
Central 1 captures images of deposit slips and deposit items and makes them available to financial institutions via the Online Return System (ORS). Financial institutions use ORS to search for, view, and print Branch Capture deposits, Corporate Capture deposits, and mobile deposits. Although most inquiries can be resolved via ORS, your financial institution can submit a trace request to Central 1 if required.
16.2	Viewing and Printing Deposits via ORS
Financial institutions use ORS to search for, view, and print deposit items and deposit slips. ORS retains a history of cheque images for seven years. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures on using ORS.
16.3	Initiating Deposit Items and Financial Adjustments Trace Requests via ServiceNow
Use ServiceNow to initiate a trace request in response to an inquiry on a deposit item that can芒聙聶t be resolved via ORS or when a financial adjustment is required. If possible, use ORS to resolve inquiries and print copies because it芒聙聶s faster and more cost-effective than sending a trace request to Central 1.
When completing the trace request on ServiceNow, attach copies of items, listings, supporting documentation, and/or reports related to the inquiry, if applicable, to the ServiceNow ticket.
For qualified clearing items, attach a copy of your financial institution芒聙聶s clearing report (e.g., CLI, CLO, CLUS) that captures the items.
For unqualified clearing items, attach a copy of the debit/credit that Central 1 issued for the items.
For deposit items, attach a copy of the deposit slip or virtual deposit slip and teller tape listing with the deposit total.
For other statement items, excluding electronic forms, attach a copy of your financial institution芒聙聶s clearing report (e.g., CLI, CLO, CLUS) or a Central聽1 statement that captures the items.
To initiate a trace request on ServiceNow for deposit items and financial adjustments:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select

Note: 	Mandatory fields have an asterisk (*).
芒聭

Use the drop-down menu to select the type of trace (e.g., copy required, chargeback). Depending on the trace request you selected, additional fields may require completion (e.g., Other).

芒聭娄

. The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created.
Figure 21: Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request Form
Once Central 1 completes the trace request, a response is provided via ServiceNow. If applicable, a service charge is debited to your financial institution芒聙聶s account.
Important
:	Always log off the Client Centre and close the browser when you step away from your desk or when you芒聙聶re finished using the system.
16.4	Initiating Deposit Items and Financial Adjustments Trace Requests Manually
If ServiceNow is unavailable, complete the Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request, Form 1550, to initiate a trace request for deposit items and financial adjustments. Central 1 will only accept this form if ServiceNow is unavailable.
Prior to initiating trace requests for deposit items or financial adjustments, review the Section 16.3, Initiating Deposit Items and Financial Adjustments Trace Requests via ServiceNow, for information regarding supporting documentation and trace requests.
If ServiceNow is unavailable:
Complete the Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request, Form 1550. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 22.
Figure 22: Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request, Form 1550
Send the form and supporting documentation, if applicable, to Central 1 as directed on the form.
Wait for Central 1芒聙聶s response. Inquiries that require additional information and/or responses from a third party will take longer to process.
Once Central 1 completes the trace request, a response is sent to your financial institution. If applicable, a service charge is debited to your financial institution芒聙聶s account. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725, for service fees.
Related Information
